clusters.label: Forest Landscape Restoration Assessment and Governance
clusters.description: This cluster represents practical methodologies and analytical frameworks for assessing restoration opportunities, monitoring implementation, and mapping the social networks and actors that drive landscape restoration outcomes.
key_items: Restoration Opportunities Assessment Methodology (ROAM) [ks_006]
Collect Earth mapathons for monitoring forest and landscape restoration [ks_053, ks_074]
Social network analysis (SNA) for mapping restoration actor networks and governance [ks_007, ks_052]
Restoration Diagnostic — three-step assessment of key success factors for FLR [ks_088]
Restoration Launchpad — step-by-step guidebook synthesizing WRI restoration methodology [ks_072]
InVEST ecosystem service model [ks_065]
Co$ting Nature ecosystem service model [ks_065]
WaterWorld ecosystem service model [ks_065]
ARIES ecosystem service model [ks_065]
LPJ-GUESS ecosystem service model [ks_065]
TerraMatch / Restore Local — unified monitoring, reporting, and verification (MRV) framework for African restoration projects [ks_084, ks_092]
Sustainability Index for Landscape Restoration (SILR) — tool for monitoring biophysical and socioeconomic restoration impacts [ks_078]
Transfer learning CNN for classifying natural, monoculture, and agroforestry tree-based systems from remote sensing imagery [ks_093]
Neural information retrieval architecture for classifying FLR policy agenda across policy documents [ks_008]

clusters.label: Farmer-Led Restoration Finance and Scale
clusters.description: This cluster represents knowledge about financing mechanisms, business models, and policy instruments that enable smallholder farmers and restoration entrepreneurs across Africa and Latin America to restore degraded landscapes while generating livelihoods and achieving conservation outcomes at scale.
key_items: KOFAR restoration enterprise in Kenya [ks_017]
Lentera Africa restoration business [ks_017]
TerraFund for AFR100 project cohort with $15M investment across 27 countries [ks_032]
Farmer-managed natural regeneration (FMNR) in the Sahel [ks_031, ks_034]
Paran Women Group collective of 64 Indigenous women's groups in Kenya [ks_022]
Payment for Ecosystem Services (PES) as government policy mechanism [ks_082, ks_003, ks_048, ks_050]
Guatemala's PINFOR forestry incentive program (1996-2016) [ks_050]
Guatemala's PINPEP program for smallholder enrollment [ks_050]
Guatemala's PROBOSQUE forestry program (succeeding PINFOR through 2046) [ks_050]
Costa Rica's PPSA Payment for Ecosystem Services Program established 1996 [ks_048]
Mexico's CONAFOR national PES system with three federal schemes [ks_003]
Local radio peer-to-peer farmer knowledge sharing for FMNR diffusion in Niger [ks_034]
Agroforestry transition from cassava monoculture in Juruti, Pará, Brazil [ks_030]
Native species cultivation for traditional medicine livelihoods using Vernonia amygdalina in Rwanda [ks_040]

clusters.label: Economic Viability of Landscape Restoration
clusters.description: This cluster synthesizes evidence on the financial returns, cost-benefit analyses, and economic incentives for scaling tree-based restoration, agroforestry, and tenure security across tropical regions, demonstrating that conservation and restoration investments are economically viable while addressing tenure barriers and research gaps.
key_items: MapTenure web-based platform for tenure clarity in Central India [ks_054]
Trees Outside Forests (ToF) scaling policy framework across six Indian states [ks_075]
Community forest tenure security benefit-cost analysis methodology [ks_004]
VERENA Investment Tool for native tree species and agroforestry valuation [ks_068]
Collect Earth mapathons for landscape restoration diagnostics [ks_073]
Pre-competitive R&D Platform for native species silviculture with ROI scenarios [ks_045]
Enrichment planting of naturally regenerating forests with native non-timber products [ks_049]
Matching analysis for quantifying avoided deforestation and carbon benefits from Indigenous land rights [ks_005]
Six economic and political economy barriers framework for forest conservation and restoration scaling [ks_070]
Native-species reforestation economic models evaluation (40 models across silviculture, agroforestry, and crop-livestock-forest integration) [ks_087]
Assisted Natural Regeneration (ANR) acceleration practices and field implementation [ks_059, ks_094]
CHARM model for projecting GHG consequences of competing land-use pathways [ks_079]
Payments for ecosystem services mechanisms for Indigenous peoples and local communities climate finance access [ks_061]

clusters.label: Monitoring and Measuring Tree Restoration at Scale
clusters.description: This cluster addresses the technical, methodological, and practical challenges of tracking, verifying, and implementing large-scale tree restoration initiatives through satellite monitoring, ground-truthing, and stakeholder engagement while ensuring ecological and social integrity.
key_items: Trillion Trees initiative [ks_019, ks_042]
TerraMatch platform [ks_025]
Initiative 20x20 [ks_086]
Global Forest Watch [ks_020]
Collect Earth tool [ks_020, ks_028]
Priceless Planet Coalition [ks_042]
REDD+ mechanism in Paris Agreement Article 5 [ks_023]
WRI Restoration Diagnostic [ks_026]
University of Maryland satellite data on tree cover [ks_086]
Indigenous and Community Lands carbon report by RRI/Woods Hole/WRI/EDF [ks_037]
WRI Restoration Global Tour series [ks_026]
Tijuca urban forest restoration project in Rio de Janeiro [ks_026]
Atlantic Forest protection and restoration efforts in Salvador and São Paulo [ks_014]

clusters.label: Climate-Resilient Agriculture and Nature-Based Solutions
clusters.description: This cluster synthesizes evidence and actionable guidance on transformative agricultural adaptation and nature-based solutions for building food security and climate resilience, targeting policymakers, funders, researchers, and financial institutions across Sub-Saharan Africa and globally.
key_items: Farmer-managed natural regeneration (FMNR) [ks_009]
Integrated crop-livestock-forestry systems [ks_012]
Agroforestry [ks_012]
Direct seeding [ks_012]
Rainwater harvesting [ks_091, ks_012]
Tree-based agricultural systems [ks_009]
INSPIRE framework for Nature-Based Solutions investment [ks_062]
Glasgow Declaration on nature-based solutions [ks_085]
Adaptation Fund pledges for nature-based solutions [ks_085]
Gender-responsive agricultural adaptation financing mechanisms [ks_064]
Transformative agricultural adaptation approaches for smallholder farmers [ks_002, ks_055, ks_056, ks_063]
Nature-Based Solutions scaling strategies across Sub-Saharan African ecosystems [ks_091]

clusters.label: Scaling Restoration Through Policy, Technology, and Business
clusters.description: This cluster represents knowledge on how landscape restoration can be rapidly scaled to meet global commitments (350 Mha by 2030) through aligned policy frameworks, technological innovation for site selection, and economic models that create jobs and revenue while addressing enforcement barriers and land tenure challenges.
key_items: DINOv3-based remote sensing AI model for identifying tree regrowth and directing restoration finance [ks_080]
Bonn Challenge restoration commitments aligned with Nationally Determined Contributions under the Paris Agreement [ks_016]
African Forest Landscape Restoration Initiative (AFR100) with commitments from 21 nations covering 63.3 million hectares [ks_015]
Multi-stakeholder platforms (MSPs) for landscape restoration coordination, implemented in Rwanda and Malawi within AFR100 [ks_033]
Federal investment program of $4–4.5 billion annually in US tree restoration to create 150,000+ jobs [ks_089]
Natural regeneration management approach as alternative to large-scale tree-planting for meeting restoration targets [ks_018]
Restoration enterprise business model linking company value propositions directly or indirectly to restoration outcomes [ks_024, ks_035]
Degraded land use for agricultural expansion as alternative to deforestation, supporting food security and rural livelihoods [ks_038]
Landscapes of Opportunity 2011 global assessment framework for forest landscape restoration planning [ks_043]
Three-step landscape restoration methodology: land assessment with stakeholder planning, realistic target-setting and capacity building, monitoring and learning [ks_029]
Five government finance levers: carbon pricing, incentive shifts, risk mitigation, land use planning, and rigorous accounting [ks_036]
Forest laws reform addressing tenure clarity and enforcement consistency to enable rather than block restoration [ks_011]
Policy coherence framework across sectors, governance levels, and time for effective landscape and seascape restoration [ks_081]

clusters.label: Financial Instruments & Capital Mobilization
clusters.description: This cluster synthesizes evidence on the economic case for restoration, practical implementation pathways, and mechanisms—from public incentives to private investment—for financing and scaling land and forest restoration at regional and national levels.

Project aggregation into funds and securities [ks_001]
Four-step roadmap for attracting private capital: investor engagement, business planning, entity structuring, and performance tracking [ks_046]
Initiative 20x20 restoration target in Latin America and the Caribbean [ks_058]
